rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 1. SETTLEMENT COMPLIANCE HEARING. Hearing required. The court's minutes for January 23, 2025 state that the plaintiff's counsel is to file a declaration no later than February 3, 2025, No declaration was received. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STUART VIDANAGE VS. JONATHAN PARKHURST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 2 DEFENDANT BAY AREA MEN'S SENIOR BASEBALL LEAGUE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Bay Area Men's Senior Baseball League's motion for summary judgment is denied. The League did not satisfy its initial summary judgment burden of presenting admissible evidence showing that it did not act grossly negligently regarding the incident that assertedly harmed plaintiff Stuart Vidnage. The League's reply memorandum acknowledges that the release of liability provision is not a defense to gross negligence, yet nowhere in the League's separate statement are there any facts showing that the League did not act grossly negligent. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE E. LOPEZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO ANIMAL MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE LOPEZ' MOTION TO TRANSFER. Plaintiff Christine Lopez's motion to transfer actions from other counties and coordinate them for all pretrial purposes is denied. Assuming without deciding that this action and the four others which are the subject of this motion are not complex and thus a permissible motion per Code of Civil Procedure 403, Ms. Lopez has failed to show that the factors specified in Rule 3.500(d) favor her extraordinary request. The primary reasons that Ms. Lopez appears to advance to support her request that all five actions be coordinated in San Francisco for all pretrial purposes is that this Court has a civil master calendar, as opposed to direct civil calendars, and this Court has dedicated law and motion departments for non-complex to hear certain pretrial motions in cases not designated complex. While arguably these reasons relate to the factor stated in 3.500(d)(5) ("the efficient utilization of judicial facilities and staff resources"), this Court does not and will not take the position that its manner of handling civil cases or pretrial portions thereof is more efficient or superior in any way to the manner of handling such matters by its sister courts elsewhere in the state. More specifically, there is no reason to believe that the courts where the other four actions are pending cannot and will not efficiently resolve the four assertedly common questions of fact, which, per CCP 403, must necessarily not be complex. While it is possible that different trial courts will reach different decisions on one or more of these questions, doing so in different cases involving different parties is not necessarily disadvantageous and may even lead to better decision-making at the trial and appellate court level. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: XUAN JIANG VS. XIAOYIN QU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 7. 2 - DEFENDANT XIAOYIN QU's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Xiaoyin Qu's demurrer to all causes of action (the first, second, fourth, fifth, eighth, and tenth through nineteenth) in the complaint filed by Xuan Jiang is overruled as to the first cause for breach of fiduciary duty owed to defendant Run the World, the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty owed to Ms. Jiang, the tenth cause of action for work environment harassment, the fourteenth cause of action for interference with contract, the fifteenth cause of action for fraud, the sixteenth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the seventeenth cause of action for conversion, the eighteenth cause of action for trespass to chattels, and the nineteenth cause of action for misappropriation of name or likeness, and is sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for misappropriation of corporate assets, the fifth cause of action for accounting, the eighth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy, the eleventh cause of action for violation of the California Comprehensive Computer Data Access and Fraud Act, the twelfth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the thirteenth cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress, In the exercise of discretion, the court considered every word on every page of Ms. Qu's overlong initial memorandum. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. RYAN J WARMAN
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 8. 1 - DEFENDANTs RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN, PRINCIPAL AND REPRESENTED PERSON Motion To Dismiss. Defendant's motion to dismiss is denied. Because the stated grounds for the motion are, among other things, plaintiff Citibank's sole cause of action fails to state a claim, the court has treated the motion as a demurrer. The motion fails to show any defect in the complaint that warrants sustaining a demurrer. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. RYAN J WARMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Default And Request For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 8. 3 - DEFENDANTs RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN, PRINCIPAL AND REPRESENTED PERSON Notice Of Default And Request For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief. Defendant's notice of default and request for declaratory and relief is denied. The motion appears to be seeking temporary, preliminary and/or permanent injunctive relief and/or an immediate declaration, but fails to provide any competent evidence showing entitlement to such relief. rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. RYAN J WARMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Unredacted Documents Pursuant To Crc 2.550-2.551; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Defendant In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2025, Line 8. 2 - DEFENDANT RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN, PRINCIPAL AND REPRESENTED PERSON's Motion To Seal Unredacted Documents Pursuant To Crc 2.550-2.551. Defendant's motion to seal is denied. The motion fails to show any overriding interest, much less that an overriding interest would be prejudiced by public disclosure of the papers filed in this case seeking payment on a credit card debt.
